From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Jean Bogard](/w/index.php?title=Jean_Bogard&redirect=no "Jean Bogard"))

**Jean Bogard** (died around 1634) was a printer in [Leuven](/wiki/Leuven "Leuven") and [Douai](/wiki/Douai "Douai") in the 16th and 17th centuries.

## Life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jan_Bogard&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life")]

Bogard was born in Leuven around the mid-16th century and from 1564 was working as a printer in the city.[[1]](#cite_note-Reusens-1) Not long after the foundation of [Douai University](/wiki/Douai_University "Douai University") Bogard began publishing in Douai, while continuing to maintain his printing house in Leuven until around 1600.[[1]](#cite_note-Reusens-1)

Bogard died in Douai around 1634, and his business was continued by his heirs.[[1]](#cite_note-Reusens-1)

## Publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jan_Bogard&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Publications")]

* [Vincent of Lerins](/wiki/Vincent_of_Lerins "Vincent of Lerins"), *Petit traicté ... pour la vérité et antiquité de la Foy Catholique* (Leuven, 1564)[[2]](#cite_note-2)
* [Arnold Mermannus](/w/index.php?title=Arnold_Mermannus&action=edit&redlink=1 "Arnold Mermannus (page does not exist)"), *De Fugienda Consuetudine Haereticorum Oratio Paraenetica Ad Catholicos* (Leuven, 1564)[[3]](#cite_note-3)
* [Petrus Bacherius](/w/index.php?title=Petrus_Bacherius&action=edit&redlink=1 "Petrus Bacherius (page does not exist)"), *Hortulus precationum* (Leuven, 1566)
* [François Richardot](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Richardot "François Richardot"), *Quatre sermons du sacrement de l'autel* (Leuven, 1567)[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* Petrus Bacherius, *In Omnes Epistolas Quadragesimales Homiliae* (Leuven, 1572)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* *Hortulus animae* (Douai, 1574)
* [François Pollet](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Pollet "François Pollet"), *Historia Fori Romani* (Douai, 1576)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* Jan Pascha, *La pérégrination spirituelle vers la Terre saincte et cité de Jérusalem*, translated by Nicolas de Leuze (Douai, 1576)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Prosper of Aquitaine](/wiki/Prosper_of_Aquitaine "Prosper of Aquitaine"), *Opera* (Douai, 1577)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* *Recueil des lettres, actes et pieces plus signalees du progres et besongne faict en la ville d'Arras & ailleurs, pour parvenir à une bonne paix & reconciliation avec sa majesté catholicque, par les estatz d'Arthois & deputez d'autres provinces* (Douai, 1579)[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* *Traicte de reconciliation faict en la ville d'Arras le XVIIe. de may 1579* (Douai, 1579)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* Jean Michel, *L'anatomie du corps politique comparé au corps humain*, translated from Latin by Paul du Mont (Douai, 1581)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* [Balthazar Ayala](/wiki/Balthazar_Ayala "Balthazar Ayala"), *De jure et officiis bellicis et disciplina militari* (Douai, 1582)
* *Statuta synodi dioecesanae audomarensis, anno M D LXXXIII* (Douai, 1583), the statutes of the diocesan synod called by [Jean Six](/wiki/Jean_Six "Jean Six") to introduce Tridentine reform in the [diocese of Saint-Omer](/wiki/Ancient_Diocese_of_Saint-Omer "Ancient Diocese of Saint-Omer")
* [Floris Van der Haer](/wiki/Floris_Van_der_Haer "Floris Van der Haer"), *De initiis tumultuum Belgicorum* (Douai, 1587)[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* [Guillaume Gazet](/wiki/Guillaume_Gazet "Guillaume Gazet"), *Magdalis. Comoedia sacra* (Douai, 1589)
* [Luis de Granada](/wiki/Luis_de_Granada "Luis de Granada"), *Le memorial de la vie chrestienne* (Douai, 1592)[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* [Estienne Du Tronchet](/wiki/Estienne_Du_Tronchet "Estienne Du Tronchet"), *Lettres missives et familières d'Estienne du Tronchet* (Leuven, 1593)[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* [Giovanni Pietro Maffei](/wiki/Giovanni_Pietro_Maffei "Giovanni Pietro Maffei"), *Les trois livres de la vie du Père Ignace de Loyole*, translated by [Michel d'Esne](/wiki/Michel_d%27Esne "Michel d'Esne") (Douai, 1594)[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* [Joannes Molanus](/wiki/Joannes_Molanus "Joannes Molanus"), *De Historia SS. Imaginum et Picturarum pro vero earum usu* (Leuven, 1594) [Available on Google Books](https://books.google.com/books?id=NkFYH8uo0W4C)
* *Lettre du Japon des années 1591 et 1592*, translated by Michel d'Esne (Douai, 1595)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* [Fulvio Androzzi](/wiki/Fulvio_Androzzi "Fulvio Androzzi"), *Traictè de la frequente communion et des fruicts qui en procedent*, translated by [Antoine Gazet](/wiki/Antoine_Gazet "Antoine Gazet") (Douai, 1599)[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* [Joannes Busaeus](/wiki/Joannes_Busaeus "Joannes Busaeus"), *Manuel contenant des dévotes méditations sur tous les évangiles* (Douai, 1612)
* [Baldric of Noyon](/wiki/Baldric_of_Noyon "Baldric of Noyon"), *Chronicon Cameracense et Atrebatense*, edited by Georges Colvener (Douai, 1615)[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* [Jean-Baptiste Gramaye](/wiki/Jean-Baptiste_Gramaye "Jean-Baptiste Gramaye"), *Rerum Duacensium Libri Tres* (Douai, 1618)[[19]](#cite_note-19)
